measured.transcript: We will hear argument this morning in case twenty two four Mr. Waxman. Richard Glossop was convicted on the word of one man, Justin Sneed, the undisputed in this case. Oklahoma has now disclosed evidence revealing that Mr Sneed lied to the jury about his history psychiatric treatment, including the fact that a prison psychiatrist prescribed lithium to treat his previously undiagnosed disorder. The prosecution suppressed that evidence and then fail to correct Mr Sneed's perjured denial, just as it suppressed evidence that in the middle of trial, in violation of the Court's sequestration order, Sneed altered his testimony about the knife on the victim at the urgent request of the prosecutor, who then falsely denied to the Court her prior knowledge. There is no adequate or independent jurisdictional bar to review and no warrant for an hearing. As to independence, the opinion is suffused with merit's determination on the Brady and Napo claims, and certainly there is no clear express statement that the Court's is based on a bona separate, adequate and independent grounds as long by this Court to preclude review, is there any adequate bar. By the State's waiver, the Court a jurisdictional threshold it had never in any other case. The disposition was, quote, without support in prior State law, as required over sixty years of this Court's precedent to establish adequacy. No evidentiary hearing could alter the conclusion Mr Glossop was denied due process. There is no dispute that, contrary to Sneed's sworn testimony, the State's own record shows that he was in fact treated by for bipolar disorder, just as there is no dispute that Sneed changed his testimony about the knife. at the urgent mid trial request of the prosecutor, who then falsely denied that very fact to the court. this Court should reverse and remand for a new trial. I welcome the Court's questions. Mr Waxman, you place quite a bit of weight on the The note uh Notes. from And From your opening statement. agree with them. Did you at any point get a statement from either one of the prosecutors? Yes. Well to be clear We we did get a sworn statement, which I believe is at page nine hundred sixty of the joint appendix from Gary Ackley. in which for among other things he never mentions the account that he has now provided to the in Amicus brief for the Van family. to Ms. Smotherman, Ms. Smotherman was interviewed both by the independent counsel and by the Attorney General's independent counsel, Mr Duncan, she gave different answers each time, none of which was the account she has now provided in an letter attached to the Van Trees Well it would seem that because not only reputations are being impugned, but are central to this case. it would seem that an interview of these two prosecutors would be central. suggest that their interviews were generally about prosecuting capital cases and not specifically about the details of this. appended at the very last minute for the very first time. in a merits amicus brief before this Court. benefit of the doubt. That they you know, to which they are entitled. When in the context of Mr Ackley did file an affidavit It is in the record in this case. And it is not in any way consistent with his current account. Well he he made himself available. He provided us a declaration No, what you are saying make sense if for some reason they had made themselves unavailable, they suggest that they were not sought out and given an opportunity.
